sections.paragraph:
ἀμύνομαι fut. 3 sg. ἀμυνεῖται Tat. 17:4, 1 aor. ἠμυνάμην (Hom.+; LXX, JosAs, ApcrEzk [Epiph. 70, 8]; but here just as rare as in the ins [e.g. SIG 780, 35; IAndrosIsis, Kyme 37 p. 124 P.] and the pap [e.g. APF 3, 1906, p. 418, 71]—Jos., Bell. 1, 319; 5, 129, Ant. 9, 9 al.; Tat. 17, 2; 4) to help by coming to the aid of, help, assist, defend someone (Is 59:16; JosAs 23:11 cod. A [p. 75, 9 Bat.]) abs. w. ἐκδίκησιν ποιεῖν of Moses, who killed an Egyptian Ac 7:24 (for Att. ἀμύνω B-D-F §316, 1). This mng. is perh. preferable to retaliate (as Epict. 4, 13, 7; JosAs 24:7).—DELG s.v. ἀμύνω. M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

aor. aor. = aorist

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

ApcrEzk ApcrEzk = Apocryphon Ezechiel—List 2

Epiph Epiph , IV–V a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

IAndrosIsis IAndrosIsis = Der Isishymnus von Andros und verwandte Texte—List 3

Kyme Kyme = text fr. Kyme

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

APF APF = Archiv für Papyrusforschung—Lists 4, 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

abs. abs. = absolute

Att. Att. = Attic, esp. in ref. to dialect

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

perh. perh. = perhaps

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 55
